_doc: Course 1 PREMIER INTRO — 'What Marketing Actually Is' (no-bullshit edition). Locked doodle master style. Exact narration from booked-course1-intro-narration.md, single narrator (Torque Marshal). ~4 min.

scenes.vo: Let's get one thing straight before we start. You didn't get into this trade to become a marketer. You got in to do good work, get paid, and go home.
So when some guy in a vest shows up talking about brand synergy and engagement, your bullshit detector goes off. Good. It should.
Most of what gets sold to you as marketing is decoration. Pretty logos and cute little posts that nobody ever booked a job from. We're not doing that here.

Here's what marketing actually is, with the crap scraped off. Marketing is the machine that takes a stranger with a problem, a busted water heater, a leaking roof, a dead A C in the middle of July, and turns them into a paid job on your calendar.

scenes.text: GET FOUND GET PICKED GET BOOKED
scenes.vo: That's the whole thing. Get found. Get picked. Get booked. Everything else is wallpaper.
Notice what I didn't say. I didn't say go viral. I didn't say likes. I didn't say brand awareness. You can't cash a like. You can't deposit a follower.
The only scoreboard that pays your guys on Friday is booked jobs. That's literally why this is called Booked. I don't care if you're famous. I care if your calendar's full.

Now here's the good news, and it's the most important thing I'll say all day. You do not have to become a marketing genius. You'll never out-nerd the agencies, and you don't need to. You just need to know enough to not get fleeced.
Because right now, if a marketer walks in and starts slinging words at you, you've got two moves. Nod along and hope, or hand over a card and pray. Both of those end with you broke and pissed off.
By the time we're done, you'll know which questions to ask, which numbers to demand, and exactly when somebody's blowing smoke up your ass.

And it all comes down to two numbers. What it costs you to get a lead, and what it costs you to get a booked job. Those are not the same number, and mixing them up is exactly how contractors go broke buying cheap leads.

So write this on the wall. A cheap lead that never books is the most expensive lead there is. You'll pay twenty bucks for a lead that ghosts you and feel like a genius. Meanwhile the guy paying eighty for a lead that actually books is eating your lunch.
We're gonna fix that thinking. That's the very next course, Know Your Numbers, and it's the most important one in here.

So here's where we're headed. First we figure out where customers actually find you, and there are really only five doors they come through. Your Google profile and the map. Your website. Paid ads. Lead networks like Angi and Thumbtack. And your reviews.
Some of those doors are free. Some of them cost real money. And most contractors ignore the free ones and overpay for the rest. Backwards.
Then we go door by door. We claim and fix the most valuable free thing you own, your Google Business Profile, because that little map at the top of the search beats everybody sitting under it.

We tackle your website, which has exactly one job. Turn a visitor into a phone call. Most contractor sites convert two, maybe three percent. Ninety-eight out of a hundred people show up and leave like ghosts. Pretty doesn't pay. Working pays.
We get into reviews, because your reputation is straight-up currency now. Nine out of ten people read 'em before they ever call you, and most won't even consider you under four stars.
We get honest about paid leads, when they're worth it, and when they're a trap. We spend your first ad dollar without lighting it on fire.
We get you found by Google and by A I, because about a third of people now start with a chatbot instead of a search bar.
And we finish with your scorecard, the handful of numbers you check every month so you're the one directing your marketing, instead of getting robbed by it.

scenes.text: GET FOUND GET PICKED GET BOOKED
scenes.vo: That's the whole game. Get found. Get picked. Get booked. No jargon, no vests, no bullshit.
You don't have to do everything. You just have to understand enough to make smart calls and put your money where it actually comes back.
So that's the deal. Next up, Know Your Numbers, C P L and C P B J, the two numbers that decide whether your marketing makes you money or quietly bleeds you dry. Grab a coffee. Let's go.
Booked. Honest marketing for the trades. Get the whole course at booked job dot com.
